Project Name: Introduction to Azure Networking Scenario Design & Deployment guides Guide - content breakdown, planning, and integration into doc set
What problem does this address?: Customers new to Azure networking have no scenario-based guide to help them select and compose the right services for their business — existing docs are service-centric and procedural, not design-led. A network admin designing for SMB has different needs than one building for enterprise hybrid or multicloud, but all roads lead to the same unconnected service articles
ACC Initiative: Help customers make good decisions about technology
Detailed description of the problem: Co-author the Azure Networking Plan and Design Guide with Michael Bender — a modular, capability-first reference guide of 18 articles (1 hub + 17 capability modules) targeting Azure networking beginners through intermediate architects. Organized by capability area (Foundational, Connectivity, Topology, Security, Operations), not service. PM team (India) review and sign-off required before publishing each phase. Duong owns 9 articles: C1 Hybrid, C2 Ingress, C3 Egress, T1 Flat, T2 Hub-spoke, S1 Firewall, S2 WAF, S3 DDoS, S4 DNS.
How will you measure impact: Page views on new design articles, coverage of design-decision content gaps, positive feedback from architects and network admins; target: all 18 articles live on Learn by June 30, 2026
Baseline metrics: No dedicated design/planning guide articles currently published in azure-docs-pr for Azure Networking. No scenario-first entry point exists today. Existing docs are service-specific how-tos only.
Status: In progress — spec complete, collaboration proposal written, awaiting PM team confirmation
Signals: Customers navigate Azure Networking docs by service, not workload need; no "how do I design a network for my workload?" entry point; AAC and CAF cross-links point to this gap
In Scope: 18 articles: Hub (Michael), F1–F3 Foundational (Michael), C1–C6 Connectivity (Duong: C1–C3; Michael: C4–C6), T1–T4 Topology (Duong: T1–T2; Michael: T3–T4), S1–S4 Security (all Duong), O1–O2 Operations (all Michael)
Dependencies: PM team (India) review and sign-off per phase — Phase 0 confirmation due April 8; Michael Bender co-authorship confirmed
Risks: PM team confirmation still pending — Phase 1 starts April 10; delay in PM sign-off could block publish; 9 articles for Duong concentrated in May–June (peak load)
Timeline: une 30, 2026 (all 18 articles live). Phase 0: Apr 10 · Phase 1: Apr 30 · Phase 2: May 23 · Phase 3: Jun 6 · Phase 4: Jun 25 · Phase 5: Jun 30
Notes: Collaboration proposal and article map/spec complete (April 3, 2026). PM team (India) confirmation due April 8. Source files at C:\GitHub\Azure Networking Design\v4-modular-reference.
